summary: Sprint focused on clearing App Store submission blockers: ecosystem sync gaps, podcast sync, and privacy policy hosting.

cro.description: Growth strategy, monetization. Gazelle-intense focus on revenue.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.
concerns: 1 podcast episode(s) not synced to website

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Evening team. Let's make this sprint count. Quick state of play: build is healthy, 720 commits in seven days which is exceptional velocity. We have one uncommitted hub change to clean up. Three critical sync gaps remain — the podcast MDX mismatch, the missing blog post on the website, and App Store submission still pending. Last standup identified the privacy policy and terms of service as a hidden blocker. CEO already decided we draft and host those tonight using a simple template. Let's hear where we are and what ships in the next fifteen minutes. Elon (CTO): The one uncommitted hub file is a data update — trivial, I'll commit that in the first two minutes of this sprint. On the three critical sync gaps, the podcast MDX mismatch is the fastest win: we have one episode produced but not synced to the website. That's a content-side task more than engineering. The blog post rendering issue and the App Store submission are the real blockers. I can address the ecosystem sync gaps and knock out the five attention items in review this sprint. Privacy policy and ToS — I need to know: are we hosting these as static pages on joshlevylabs.com or within the Lever app bundle itself?

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: I'll own syncing that missing podcast episode to the website — it's content packaging, not code. That clears one of our three critical sync gaps tonight. On faith content, we're at 7 lessons against the CEO's target of 15-20. I can draft additional lesson content this sprint to push us closer. The daily devotional pipeline in A-005 feeds into this same body of work, so I'm treating them as one effort. Also flagging: Jett's vaccine is tomorrow, February 20th — that reminder is queued and ready.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue reality check: we have zero active revenue streams. The App Store submission is the single biggest unlock for monetization. Every sprint hour not moving toward that launch is a sprint hour wasted. The CEO said launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on. I'd like to start sketching that paid tier structure so the moment we're live, we can flip the switch. On idle cash — CEO said keep it liquid, J-001 stays in monitoring mode. Fear and Greed at 39, still in fear territory, which validates that call.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The product is four tabs, seven faith lessons, and it works. That's the submission scope — the CEO was clear. What's actually blocking us from submitting tonight? It's two things: the privacy policy URL and the App Store listing assets — screenshots, description, keywords. The privacy policy is a template job, thirty minutes tops. The listing assets are what we should be obsessing over because that's the first impression. Every word in that App Store description needs to be deliberate.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: From an instrumentation standpoint, once we're on the App Store, we need to know what's happening inside the app. Are we capturing session data, lesson completion rates, tab navigation patterns? If we launch without measurement, we're flying blind. That said, I agree with Steve — don't let instrumentation block the launch. Ship it, then instrument it in the next sprint cycle.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Market context is straightforward. SPY is sideways, Fear and Greed at 39, no buy signals today. The four positions in XLV, AEE, EWA, and AKAM are all holds. Paper equity sitting at just under $100K with $75K cash. Nothing demands action on the trading side tonight. The real financial priority is getting that app live so we have a product in market.

transcript.speaker: faith
transcript.message: The seven lessons we have cover foundational ground across the eight dimensions, but there are gaps. Some dimensions only have one lesson. The CEO wants two to three per dimension, so we need at least nine more. Each lesson needs to be substantive — not filler. I'd rather ship with seven strong lessons and add depth post-launch than rush thin content to hit a number.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good point, faith. Let me get one round of cross-talk before we close. Elon (CTO): Steve, to answer your question on what's blocking — the privacy policy hosting location matters. If we put it on joshlevylabs.com as a static page, I can handle the routing tonight. That's my recommendation. App Store assets though — screenshots and description — that's a Joshua call. He needs to approve what we put in front of Apple.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Agreed on hosting at joshlevylabs.com. And faith is right — don't dilute the seven lessons we have by rushing nine more. Launch with seven. The content depth is a post-launch sprint. Tonight's energy goes to removing submission blockers, period.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alignment confirmed. Here's the sprint priority stack. First: Elon addresses the three critical ecosystem sync gaps including committing that hub change — that's A-001 and the existing tickets. Second: Alex syncs the missing podcast episode to the website, which directly clears a critical gap. Third: Alex continues drafting faith lesson content toward the 15-20 target, understanding we may launch with seven and backfill. The privacy policy and App Store listing assets are the real submission blockers — we need a CEO decision on whether to prioritize those tonight or hold for the next cycle. One question for Joshua.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

Joshua's made the call — privacy policy and App Store listing assets ship tonight. That means the critical path to submission is fully active this sprint, no more deferrals. Every blocker we've been circling is now an action item with an owner.

Updated sprint priority stack: **First**, Elon commits the hub change, resolves ecosystem sync gaps, and stands up the privacy policy as a static page on joshlevylabs.com — that's the fastest submission unblock. **Secon

actionItems.text: Address 3 critical ecosystem sync gaps (podcast MDX mismatch, blog post rendering, App Store submission prep)
Sync 1 podcast episode to website to close content gap
Review 5 ecosystem attention items with architecture specs
Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons, target 15-20)
Evaluate deployment of idle cash ($75,340 available) — continue monitoring, no action per CEO directive

topPriorities: Address 3 critical ecosystem sync gaps to unblock App Store submission
Sync missing podcast episode to website to close content gap
Draft additional faith lesson content toward 15-20 lesson target

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

ceoDecisions.question: Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
options: Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Draft privacy policy tonight but defer listing assets to tomorrow's sprint
Defer both to tomorrow — focus tonight on sync gaps and content only
